findings.evidence: return Double.parseDouble(input)
findings.fix: Parse into BigDecimal via new BigDecimal(input), never a double money value.

findings.evidence: private double amount;
findings.fix: Type the entity money field as BigDecimal so it maps to a decimal column.

findings.evidence: private double amount;
findings.fix: Type the amount field as BigDecimal, not double.

findings.evidence: subtotal DOUBLE PRECISION (also tax, total, unit_price REAL, payments/webhook/ledger amount)
findings.fix: Use NUMERIC/DECIMAL for money columns, never DOUBLE PRECISION or REAL.

findings.evidence: precision = 6, scale = 2 on balance
findings.fix: Size precision for the largest balance accumulator, e.g. precision 19 scale 4.

findings.evidence: balance NUMERIC(6, 2)
findings.fix: Widen to NUMERIC(19,4) or similar so a running balance cannot overflow.

findings.evidence: Math.round(amount * 100) and minor / 100.0, unit only in the name
findings.fix: Use a Money value type carrying amount plus currency; keep conversion at the boundary only.

findings.evidence: balance and every amount column with no currency_code column
findings.fix: Add an ISO 4217 currency_code column next to each amount.

findings.evidence: BigDecimal balance with no adjacent currency field
findings.fix: Bind amount and currency in a Money value type or add a currencyCode field.

findings.evidence: amount field with no adjacent currency
findings.fix: Store a currency alongside the amount or use a Money value type.

findings.evidence: amount field with no adjacent currency
findings.fix: Carry the currency with the amount, e.g. a currencyCode column.

findings.evidence: Math.round(value * 100) / 100.0, hardcoded two decimals
findings.fix: Drive scale from the currency ISO 4217 minor-unit exponent, not a hardcoded 100.

findings.evidence: new BigDecimal(amount) where amount is a double
findings.fix: Build from a String or use BigDecimal.valueOf(amount); never new BigDecimal(double).

findings.evidence: Math.round(value * 100) / 100.0
findings.fix: Use BigDecimal.setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP) with an explicit mode, not Math.round on a double.

findings.evidence: Math.round(amount * 100)
findings.fix: Convert to minor units via BigDecimal with an explicit RoundingMode rather than Math.round on a binary float.

findings.evidence: parts[i] = Money.roundMoney(total * weights[i] / weightSum) in a loop, no residual reconciliation
findings.fix: Use largest-remainder allocation in integer minor units so sum(parts) == total by construction.

findings.evidence: discounted = roundMoney(subtotal * (1-discount)) then taxed = roundMoney(discounted * (1+tax))
findings.fix: Carry full precision through discount and tax, round once at the settlement boundary.

findings.evidence: roundMoney(unitPriceFromBundle(bundleTotal, quantity) * quantity) rounds the per-unit price then multiplies
findings.fix: Compute bundleTotal directly, do not round the per-unit figure before multiplying back by quantity.

findings.evidence: store.creditAccount(...) then events.save(...), no existsByEventId dedup before crediting
findings.fix: Look up the event id (existsByEventId) and skip the credit if already processed, inside one transaction with the marker insert.

findings.evidence: POST /v1/charges in a retry loop with no Idempotency-Key header
findings.fix: Mint one idempotency key per charge, persist it, and send it as an Idempotency-Key header on every retry.

findings.evidence: account.getBalance().add(amount); account.setBalance(...); accounts.save(account)
findings.fix: Credit with one atomic UPDATE (balance = balance + :amt) or add a @Version / PESSIMISTIC_WRITE lock.

findings.evidence: account.getBalance().subtract(amount); account.setBalance(...); accounts.save(account)
findings.fix: Debit with one atomic UPDATE (SET balance = balance - :amt WHERE balance >= :amt) and treat 0 rows as insufficient funds.

findings.evidence: event_id VARCHAR(255), nullable, no UNIQUE on the webhook_events dedup column
findings.fix: Make it event_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE so the DB is the final dedup backstop.

findings.evidence: external_ref VARCHAR(255), nullable, no UNIQUE on the payments external reference
findings.fix: Add a UNIQUE constraint (NOT NULL) on payments.external_ref to reject duplicate provider references.

findings.evidence: @Column(name = "event_id") on eventId with no unique = true
findings.fix: Add unique = true to the event_id @Column so JPA DDL and the DB enforce dedup.

findings.evidence: entry.setAmount(newAmount); entries.save(entry);
findings.fix: Leave the posted entry untouched and append a reversing entry plus a corrected entry, do not mutate and save.

findings.evidence: postEntry saves one signed leg after a single debit OR credit, no paired counter-leg
findings.fix: Record two entries per movement sharing a transactionId, a debit and a credit, and assert sum(debit) == sum(credit) before commit.

findings.evidence: store.debitAccount / creditAccount mutate a cached balance directly, no reconciliation to entries
findings.fix: Derive the balance by summing entries, or reconcile the stored balance against sum(entries) and alert on drift.

findings.evidence: ledger_entries entity has no createdBy/actorId and no reason/source/idempotencyKey
findings.fix: Add createdBy, reason/source, and an idempotency key so each money mutation records who moved money and why.

findings.evidence: Math.round(amount * rate * 100) / 100.0 rounds each leg; refundInOriginalCurrency reconverts
findings.fix: Treat conversion as one-way and lossy; fetch an independent inverse quote and never expect A->B->A to reconcile.

findings.evidence: RATES.get(...) applied and only the converted double returned; no rate value, source, or timestamp captured
findings.fix: Persist the rate value, source, and as-of timestamp alongside the amount as an immutable point-in-time fact.

findings.evidence: invoice.total = convert(...); invoice.currency = payoutCurrency overwrites in place, original amount/currency lost
findings.fix: Keep original amount and currency as the source of truth; store the converted figure as an added, separate field.

findings.evidence: total += invoice.total sums FxInvoice totals across differing currencies with no per-currency bucket
findings.fix: Aggregate per currency (group by invoice.currency); never sum mixed-currency amounts into one number.

findings.evidence: this.createdAt = LocalDateTime.now();
findings.fix: Store the instant as Instant.now() in a timestamptz column, not zoneless LocalDateTime.now().

findings.evidence: this.receivedAt = LocalDateTime.now();
findings.fix: Store the instant as Instant.now() in a timestamptz column, not zoneless LocalDateTime.now().

findings.evidence: issued_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
findings.fix: Use TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE (timestamptz) so the financial instant is stored as UTC.

findings.evidence: received_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL (payments)
findings.fix: Use TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE (timestamptz) for the payment instant.

findings.evidence: received_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL (webhook_events)
findings.fix: Use TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE (timestamptz) for the webhook instant.

findings.evidence: created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL (ledger_entries)
findings.fix: Use TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE (timestamptz) for the ledger-entry instant.

findings.evidence: return Math.round(seconds / 86400.0);
findings.fix: Count days with ChronoUnit.DAYS.between on zoned/local dates, not an epoch-second divide by 86400.

findings.evidence: !txn.at().isBefore(periodStart) && !txn.at().isAfter(periodEnd)
findings.fix: Use a half-open interval [start, next): !isBefore(start) && isBefore(end), so a boundary txn is counted once.

findings.evidence: double dailyRate = annualRate / 365;
findings.fix: Read the instrument's day-count convention and compute the year fraction via a convention object, not a hardcoded /365.

findings.evidence: double total = 0.0; total += amount over double[] amounts
findings.fix: Accumulate money in BigDecimal (or sum long minor units), not a double accumulator.

findings.evidence: totalAllPages loops fetchPage.fetch(pageSize, offset) with offset += pageSize and total += sumAmounts(amounts) across pages
findings.fix: Compute the total in one SELECT SUM query over a single snapshot, or keyset-paginate inside one repeatable-read transaction, not limit/offset page-then-add over live data.

findings.evidence: invoiceTaxByLines sums roundMoney(amount*rate) per line while invoiceTaxOnTotal rounds the total once
findings.fix: Pick one configured rounding level and call the same helper on every path so sum(round(line)) and round(sum) cannot disagree by a cent.

findings.evidence: extractTaxFromGross: tax = roundMoney(gross * SALES_TAX_RATE) on an inclusive gross, rounded apart from net
findings.fix: Back tax out of the gross: net = round(gross/(1+rate)); tax = gross - net, so net + tax == gross exactly.

findings.evidence: public static final double SALES_TAX_RATE = 0.0825; rate held as a binary double, used in double multiply-then-round
findings.fix: Hold the rate as integer basis points (825) or new BigDecimal("0.0825") and do the math in BigDecimal or integer minor units.

findings.evidence: payload.put("balance", balanceCents)
findings.fix: Serialize the balance as an integer minor-unit string plus currency, not a bare JSON number that can exceed 2^53.

findings.evidence: payload.put("total", Money.fromMinorUnits(invoice.totalCents()))
findings.fix: Emit total as a decimal string or integer minor units with currency, not a double that serializes to a bare JSON number.

findings.evidence: return Double.parseDouble(input)
findings.fix: Validate a strict money grammar then build new BigDecimal(String), never Double.parseDouble on a request amount.

findings.evidence: Math.round(amount * 100); double amount vs long cents with no shared Money type or currency
findings.fix: Adopt one canonical Money shape (integer minor units plus ISO 4217 currency) across all DTOs and drop the ad hoc *100 bridge.

findings.evidence: splitProportionally(100, {1,1,2}) asserted to {25.0,25.0,50.0}, only evenly-dividing weights, no jqwik @Property/@ForAll anywhere
findings.fix: Add a jqwik @Property generating arbitrary totals and weight vectors and assert sum(parts) == total.

findings.evidence: invoiceTotal(lines, 10, 0.10) on subtotal 100 expects 99.0; round amount and 10 percent never produce a fractional cent, passes under floor/half-up/half-even alike
findings.fix: Use awkward amounts and a .xx5 tie (e.g. 8.375 percent of 19.99, split 100 three ways) and assert the specific RoundingMode.

findings.evidence: toMinorUnits(100) expects 10000, only USD across the suite, no JPY 0-decimal or BHD 3-decimal case, no negative/refund amount, no boundary magnitude
findings.fix: Parameterize over a JPY/USD/BHD currency matrix, add a negative refund case and near-zero and near-Long-limit magnitudes.
